issue: Typo \'Pacific PACELC\' — should just be PACELC; also acronyms (CAP/PACELC) deserve a one-line expansion for self-containment.
new_q: CAP theorem — when does the tradeoff actually apply?
new_a: Only during a network PARTITION: pick Availability or Consistency. On a healthy network you can have both. PACELC extends CAP by adding the steady-state Else clause: even without partitions, you trade Latency vs Consistency.

issue: \'Add N+1th node\' is ambiguous about the starting state; clarify going from N to N+1 nodes.
new_q: Consistent hashing: scaling from N to N+1 nodes — what fraction of keys remap?
new_a: Roughly 1/(N+1) of keys move to the new node. With virtual nodes, the load redistributes evenly across existing nodes instead of from a single neighbor.

issue: \'~30 days typical\' is asserted without context; webhook retry windows vary by provider (Stripe 3 days, GitHub ~8h, etc.). Generalize.
new_q: Idempotency key TTL on a webhook receiver — how to size it?
new_a: TTL must exceed the sender\'s max retry window (e.g., Stripe ~3 days, others up to weeks). Sized too short, late retries slip past the dedupe table and cause duplicate processing.

issue: RYW acronym is used in the answer without definition; minor self-containment fix.
new_a: Session pinning: route a user\'s reads to the primary for N seconds after their write, so they see their own update. Other users keep reading from replicas, preserving scale.

issue: Still accurate but missing 2025/2026 in-Postgres options (pg_duckdb, pg_mooncake, Hydra) that let you stay in Postgres for moderate analytics.
new_q: Analytics on a 1TB Postgres table — what to do?
new_a: Move the analytics workload to a columnar engine: Snowflake / ClickHouse / DuckDB / BigQuery. Row-store Postgres is poor at full-table aggregate scans. If you must stay in-Postgres, extensions like pg_duckdb or pg_mooncake bolt a columnar engine onto Postgres.

issue: Outdated as of 2025/2026: Databricks acquired Tabular (Iceberg creators) mid-2024 for >$1B; Snowflake, AWS S3 Tables, BigQuery, and Databricks all now back Iceberg. Iceberg is the clear industry standard.
new_q: Iceberg vs Delta vs Hudi — what\'s the 2026 state?
new_a: Three open table formats over Parquet adding ACID, time travel, and schema evolution to data lakes. Iceberg has won industry consolidation (Databricks acquired Tabular 2024; AWS S3 Tables, Snowflake, BigQuery all support it natively). Delta still leads inside Databricks/Spark; Hudi specializes in record-level upserts. Cross-format projects (Delta UniForm, Apache XTable) blur the lines.

issue: Question is framed as Palantir-specific but the failure pattern is generic; answer doesn\'t reflect actual 2026 Palantir-specific red flags (perks/comp, mixing up Foundry vs Gotham, surface-level \'why Palantir\').
new_q: Biggest behavioral red flags in a Palantir interview?
new_a: (1) Fake-failure dodges (\'I work too hard\', \'I don\'t really fail\'). (2) Surface-level \'why Palantir\' — citing comp/perks/\'cool problems\' instead of specifics. (3) Mixing up Foundry (commercial) and Gotham (gov). (4) Inability to justify a technical choice. Cultural-fit signals weigh heavily; they reject strong technical candidates over these.
